qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unchecked Return.answerId causes remote panic in Cap'n Proto RPC receiver
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a separate `capnp-rpc` Return-handler bounds-check panic (`questions.slots[answer_id]`) rather than the advisory's unchecked safe constructors in `capnp` that can build arbitrary words or schemas and trigger UB.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's unsafe-constructor root cause in `capnp` (`constant::Reader::get` via unchecked root access, or `StructSchema::new`/`RawStructSchema::new` via unchecked encoded schema access).
evidence: The advisory JSON names `constant::Reader::get` and `StructSchema::new` as the vulnerable safe APIs and describes UB from `PointerReader::get_root_unchecked` and unchecked schema construction.
Patch `7b981f4c75a975c80444cd38729bcdf12bf3eabf` adds `unsafe constant::Reader::new(...)`, showing the fix is to stop safe construction of unchecked constant readers.
Patch `e3aeec213e6d1b30a182bf61682a370f20d8a02c` makes `RawStructSchema::new(...)` unsafe and updates generated schema call sites, tying the advisory to unchecked schema construction in `capnp`.

`rpc_return_answerid_remote_panic` instead targets `capnp-rpc/src/rpc.rs` direct indexing on `questions.slots[answer_id]`, which is a separate denial-of-service bug and not the advisory-described unchecked safe-constructor UB in `capnp`.
